input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>A solid black background features centered text stating "Cuts Wood, Metal and Concrete!" near the top edge. Initially, the letters appear in a low-contrast, dark grey color. During the interval, the text brightness increases substantially. The characters become stark white, achieving high visibility against the black void. No other objects or changes occur within the frame.</think><answer></answer>

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] Against a solid black background, the text "Cuts Wood, Metal and Concrete!" transitions from low-contrast dark grey to stark white at the top. [2-4] The background shifts to a wooden floor surface scattered with sawdust. The top title remains. Text appears on the left: "ADD A BIT OF DEPTH AND CONTROL THE MESS AT THE SAME TIME, BY HOOKING UP A MINI VAC TO THE ATTACHMENT PORT ON THE SIDE". An inset image on the right shows a circular saw with a vacuum hose attached, highlighted by a yellow circle. [4-6] The left text updates to "ADD A WATER HOSE FOR CONCRETE" and "ADD A CO2 HOSE FOR METAL WORK". [6-12] The scene remains entirely static. The white title persists at the top. The black instructional text regarding water and CO2 hoses stays fully legible on the left. The inset photograph on the right, depicting a circular saw with an attached black hose, shows no movement against the sawdust-strewn wooden background.

[12-14] The inset saw image and instructional text vanish, replaced by centered black text reading "WOOD • CONCRETE • METAL" on the wooden background, while the top title "Cuts Wood, Metal and Concrete!" persists. [14-16] Below the material list, the phrase "ALL IN THE SAME SAW?" fades from transparent to solid black. [18-20] The top title disappears, and a new question, "CAN A HANDHELD SAW REALLY DO THAT?", fades in at the bottom. [20-26] The scene cuts to a medium shot of a man with a shaved head and goatee against a white background. He wears a dark grey t-shirt with a pocket logo. Initially gesturing with raised hands, he then clasps them in front of his torso while speaking directly to the camera.

[26-28] A man with a shaved head and goatee stands against a white background wearing a dark grey t-shirt with a pocket logo, his hands clasped in front of his torso as he speaks. [28-30] The man fades out, replaced by a wooden floor background with sawdust piles. A light wood plank graphic appears at the top reading "WOOD • CONCRETE • METAL". Below, large black text states "THIS IS ONE OF THE STRAIGHT FLUSH SAW'S BIGGEST ADVANTAGES". [30-34] The visual content remains entirely static with the wooden floor background, scattered sawdust, the horizontal plank graphic labeled "WOOD • CONCRETE • METAL" fixed at the top, and the large black text below reading "THIS IS ONE OF THE STRAIGHT FLUSH SAW'S BIGGEST ADVANTAGES" remaining fully visible and stationary.

[40-48] A dark grey handheld saw is positioned on a light wooden plank in an outdoor setting with green grass and a blurred white vehicle in the background. A hand holds the lumber steady on the right. The saw activates, advancing along the plank and generating a spray of white dust or smoke from the cutting path. The circular blade becomes visible protruding beneath the tool's base as it nears the end of the cut. [48-50] The scene transitions to a close-up of an olive-green circular saw held by a hand. The camera highlights the bevel adjustment mechanism, clearly showing a curved metal scale with degree markings ranging from 0 to 60. The tool rests on a light surface against a bright, blurred background.

recent_thinks: [34-36] The static text graphic cuts to a close-up, side-angle shot of a dark grey power saw resting on a sheet of oriented strand board. A black electrical cord trails off to the left. The background is a soft-focus blur of green trees and grass, suggesting an outdoor environment.

[36-38] The scene transitions to a red and silver circular saw featuring "SKILSAW" text on the guard. It rests on a wooden beam in an outdoor setting with blurred green grass, replacing the previous close-up of the dark grey power tool.

[38-40] The red Skilsaw is replaced by a dark grey handheld saw resting on a light wooden plank. A person's hand is visible on the right, holding the lumber steady. The background features green grass and a blurred white vehicle parked in the distance.

[50-52] The olive-green circular saw is shown in close-up, tilted to reveal the bevel adjustment scale marked with degrees from 0 to 60. A hand grips the black rear handle while the tool rests on a light surface against a bright, blurred outdoor background. The metal base plate and adjustment knob are clearly visible.

Large text on the right states "DECREASES THE NEED TO USE THE BIG FOOT BEAM SAW", accompanied by a smaller note about cutting beams bigger than 8x8.</think><answer></answer>
